content: Code-developing autonomous agents like Kiro can embed their own persistent, structured understanding of a target codebase by using LightRAG’s lightweight knowledge graph storage as a long-term memory backbone, instead of relying on the agent to hold the entire codebase context in its active context window. This split of labor lets the agent autonomously update the knowledge graph as it modifies code, while keeping the agent’s active working memory lean for complex iterative development tasks.
